channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump arrives in Washington for inaugural celebrations to mark his return to power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump arrived in Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration, a triumphant return for the Republican four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capito
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Highlights from President-elect Donald Trump's 1st Inauguration Day; what to expect from Monday
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s second inauguration is just two days away, and excitement is building in Washington, D.C., to welcome the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's influence spreads through state capitols in both substance and symbolism
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has yet to take office, but his influence already is rippling through state capitols.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges allow some Capitol riot defendants to return to DC for Trump's inauguration
item.description: Some of the same Donald Trump supporters who stormed the U.S. Capitol four years ago are allowed to return to Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands march in Washington days before Trump takes office
item.description: Thousands of people from around the United States rallied in the nation’s capital for women’s reproductive rights and other causes they believe are under threat from the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed JD Vance in the US Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed Vice President-elect JD Vance in the U.S. Senate, the state's governor announced Friday. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House reveals Biden's final list of clemency for nearly 2,5000 people, setting new presidential record
item.description: President Biden's final list of commutations was released Friday afternoon, laying out the names and registration numbers of nearly 2,500 inmates whose sentences were reduced by President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court sides with GOP, cancels special election key to House control
item.description: In late December, DFL Gov. Tim Walz set a special election for Jan. 28. But the Supreme Court sided with Republicans who said Walz acted too quickly in setting the date and postponed the election indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Blinken tells AP he's worried Trump administration may abandon key Biden foreign policy initiatives
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Outgoing Secretary of State Antony Blinken told The Associated Press that he hopes the incoming Trump administration will press forward with key points in President Joe Biden’s foreign policy, including on the Middle East and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Time's Up for TikTok
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Timeline: Key dates in the century-long battle over the Equal Rights Amendment
item.description: The struggle over the Equal Rights Amendment started more than a century ago when leading suffragist Alice Paul first proposed it shortly after the ratification of the 19th Amendment, which granted women the right to vote.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will hear case of Maryland parents who object to LGBTQ books in their kids' classes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday agreed to hear an appeal from parents in suburban Washington who want to pull their children from elementary school classes that use books featuring LGBTQ characters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Texas lawmakers urge Trump's Secretary of State pick to get tough with Mexico on water payments
item.description: Several Texas lawmakers have asked President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for Secretary to State to help urge Mexico to pay the water it owes the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court upholds law banning TikTok if it’s not sold by its Chinese parent company
item.description: The Supreme Court has unanimously upheld the federal law banning TikTok beginning Sunday unless it’s sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Renew your driver’s license from a browser? New Walz budget seeks online option
item.description: In his newly proposed budget, Gov. Tim Walz suggests allowing online renewal for driver’s licenses. Walz said the move would increase filing fee revenue, while making it more convenient for drivers.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: What’s next for Kamala Harris
item.description: Inauguration Day marks the first time in more than 20 years that Kamala Harris will not be in public office. “It is not my nature to go quietly into the night,” she told allies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Walz offers up tighter budget blueprint amid abnormal start to session
item.description: The 2025 Minnesota Legislature got a dose of normalcy when Gov. Tim Walz unveiled his blueprint for the next two-year budget. The governor also weighed in for the first time publicly on some of the chaos in the Minnesota House.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump, New York City Mayor Eric Adams to meet at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams is meeting President-elect Donald Trump on Friday at Mar-a-Lago, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Senate adds amendment to immigration bill named after Laken Riley
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday adopted the first amendment to the Laken Riley Act, as Republicans push for a legislative win to open the new Congress. The legislation in its current form would mandate federal detention of immigrants without legal status accused of theft, burglary and other related crimes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Rudy Giuliani’s Florida condo, World Series rings on the line in latest trial
item.description: Rudy Giuliani will again square off in court against two Georgia election workers Thursday, this time seeking to keep his Florida condominium and other prized possessions from the women who won a $146 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota courts may get final say in government's constitutional clash
item.description: An ugly opening to Minnesota’s legislative session and a campaign season that won’t end is fueling lawsuit after lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After half a century in politics, Biden bids farewell in an Oval Office address
item.description: President Biden delivered a farewell speech to the nation from the Oval Office tonight — a chance for him to frame his own legacy one last time before he leaves the White House.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota legislative standoff spills out of the Statehouse into the courthouse
item.description: With accusations flying over which party is ignoring state law, the fight to control the Minnesota House is shifting from lawmakers to lawyers. DFL Secretary of State Steve Simon is calling on the Minnesota Supreme Court to weigh in on who has what power.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin’s governor creates new violence-prevention office in wake of school shooting
item.description: Wisconsin’s Democratic governor on Tuesday created a new office dedicated to preventing gun violence, a month after a school shooting not far from the state Capitol and an idea that drew immediate opposition from Republicans who said it was misguided.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
